title: Iranian Supertanker Evades US Naval Blockade Carrying 1.9 Million Barrels of Crude Oil
summary: An Iranian supertanker successfully evaded the US naval blockade and reached the Asia Pacific while carrying more than 1.9 million barrels of crude oil valued at nearly $220 million, according to TankerTrackers.com.
contents:
## Iranian Supertanker Breaks US Blockade

### Evasion Details
TankerTrackers.com reported on May 3 that an Iranian supertanker successfully navigated past US naval forces and reached the Asia Pacific region. The vessel carried more than 1.9 million barrels of crude oil, valued at approximately $220 million.

### Sanctions Context
The successful transit represents a significant breach of the US naval blockade imposed on Iranian oil exports since the beginning of the US-Israel war on Iran on February 28. Iran has maintained a counter-blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closing the waterway to most non-Iranian shipping.

### Market Impact
Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov warned that continued attacks on Russia's oil infrastructure could push prices further above the current $120-per-barrel level, noting paradoxically that higher prices could result in greater revenue for Russia even with lower export volumes.

### Maritime Intelligence
The evasion highlights the ongoing cat-and-mouse dynamic between Iranian sanctions evasion networks and Western enforcement efforts. The vessel likely employed AIS transponder manipulation, flag changes, and ship-to-ship transfers — tactics previously documented in tracking Iranian crude exports.

**Source**: Al Jazeera, TankerTrackers.com
**Published**: May 3, 2026
